fingerprint: correctness:when an inconclusive condition co-occurs with a restorable ref/index mutation in the same run, the combined-precedence criterion ('all detected mutations that are classifiable as restorable are still restored before the dominant reason is recorded') contradicts the inconclusive/stash safety rule ('do not auto-restore in v1' / 'take no action that would make the dangling work harder to recover'). the contract does not specify whether restoration is suppressed when inconclusive is the dominant reason.
reason: Addressed by acceptance criterion 10: when executor_git_guard_inconclusive is among the detected classes, all restoration is suppressed and inconclusive dominates the precedence order — exactly the co-occurrence case raised here.

fingerprint: correctness:the execute clean-tree precondition assumes the guarded working tree is free of pactum's own run artifacts, but pactum writes a tracked ledger file and an untracked run directory into that tree, which would make porcelain non-empty and bail every execute attempt as inconclusive before the agent runs.
reason: Addressed by acceptance criterion 0: the clean-tree precondition excludes pactum's own state dir via pathspec ':!.heurema/', and criterion 16 tests pactum-artifact non-interference. The self-defeat against .heurema is resolved.

fingerprint: correctness:the contract requires restoring changed refs' reflogs, but the snapshot only requires storing reflog line counts or hashes, which is insufficient data to restore the original reflog contents.
reason: Addressed by acceptance criterion 6: the reflog snapshot is detection-only (entry count / tip-entry hash); ref restoration restores only the ref pointer value via update-ref, so no full reflog replay is required. The detection-vs-restore contradiction is gone.

fingerprint: scope:the scope is under-broad for the reset-hard/no-work-loss guarantee because it does not include any working-tree content backup or recovery mechanism.
reason: Addressed by acceptance criteria 7 and 8: committed/stashed work stays recoverable in dangling commits/reflog/stash, the guard never gc/prunes, and v1 honestly documents that uncommitted working-tree edits discarded by the agent's own reset --hard are not recovered; the clean-start precondition means no pre-existing uncommitted work exists to lose.
